name: Copper Basin Retreat
tagline: 5 Acres of High Desert with Mountain Views in Southern Utah
description:
Five acres of red-rock high desert outside Cedar City, Utah — gateway to three national parks. The property sits at 5,600 feet with unobstructed views of the Markagunt Plateau and distant Pine Valley Mountains. The soil is firm and sandy, dotted with juniper and pinyon pine.

Cedar City is a growing college town with full amenities just 15 minutes away. Zion National Park is an hour south, Bryce Canyon 90 minutes east, and Cedar Breaks National Monument is practically in your backyard. Power runs along the road frontage — this is a build-ready lot in one of the most scenic corridors in the American West.

details.terrain: High desert with juniper and pinyon
details.restrictions: Residential use, no junkyard or salvage

photos.alt: Red desert landscape with distant mountains
photos.caption: Views of the Markagunt Plateau from the property

photos.alt: Juniper trees on sandy desert terrain
photos.caption: Native juniper and pinyon pine dot the landscape
Spectacular sunsets over the western horizon

name: Tallgrass Prairie Homestead
tagline: 20 Acres of Rolling Kansas Prairie with Pond
description:
Twenty acres of native tallgrass prairie in the heart of the Kansas Flint Hills — one of the last intact tallgrass ecosystems on Earth. A spring-fed pond sits in the southeast corner, stocked with bluegill and channel catfish. An old stone windmill base and working cistern remain from an 1890s homestead.

The Flint Hills are hauntingly beautiful — miles of undulating grass that shifts from emerald green in spring to copper and gold in autumn. Chase County has the lowest population density in Kansas. This is real solitude, real sky, and real land at a price that's hard to believe.

details.terrain: Rolling tallgrass prairie with spring-fed pond

Flint Hills tallgrass turns copper in autumn

name: Blue Ridge Hollow
tagline: 2.5 Acres of Wooded Mountain Land in Western North Carolina
description:
Two and a half acres of mature hardwood forest in a protected hollow just outside Burnsville, North Carolina. A year-round branch stream crosses the lower portion of the property, and the upper slope opens to filtered views of the Black Mountains — home to Mount Mitchell, the highest peak east of the Mississippi.

Underground electric and fiber internet are already stubbed to the lot. The town of Burnsville is a thriving arts community with galleries, craft breweries, and a Saturday farmers market. The Blue Ridge Parkway is a 10-minute drive. This is southern Appalachian living at its finest — four seasons, cool summers, and land that feels like it's been waiting for a cabin.
